# SECTION 4 — Structural Diagrams & Schematics

## 4.1 Lightning Protection System — Functional Architecture Flow

The following Mermaid diagram maps the *interactions* of all the major components of a complete lightning protection scheme (LPS) as per IS/IEC 62305, since a literal physical sketch is impractical in text form.

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    A[Cloud Charge Accumulation] --> B[Stepped Leader Descends]
    B --> C{Strikes Interception System or Ground?}
    C -->|Hits Air Terminal| D[Air Terminal Lightning Rod]
    C -->|Hits Power Line Directly| E[Direct Stroke on Line]
    D --> F[Down Conductor]
    E --> G[Surge Arrester at Line Entry]
    F --> H[Equipotential Bonding Bar]
    G --> H
    H --> I[Earth Electrode Rod / Plate]
    I --> J[Soil Earth Mass]
    H --> K[Protected Equipment Transformer Bus Meter]
    K --> L[Continue Operation Safely]
    J --> M[Disperses Current into Ground]
    L --> N[System Returns to Normal]

    classDef strokeClass fill:#ffcccc,stroke:#cc0000,color:#000
    classDef protectionClass fill:#cce5ff,stroke:#003366,color:#000
    classDef equipmentClass fill:#ccffcc,stroke:#006600,color:#000

    class A,B,C strokeClass
    class D,E,F,G,H,I,J protectionClass
    class K,L,M,N equipmentClass
```

**Reading the diagram (KTU Exam-Ready Interpretation):**
- The **red path (A → B → C)** is the lightning stroke event — uncontrolled, dangerous, fast (~μs).
- The **blue path (D → E → F → G → H → I → J)** is the engineered *diversion* — the current is intercepted (air terminal) or caught at line entry (arrester), and routed via down-conductor to the earth mass.
- The **green path (K → L → M → N)** is the protected equipment — it sees only a small residual voltage because the bulk of the surge is shunted to earth.
